extra.reason: Central configuration layer added: PostgresOptions/AzureDocumentIntelligenceOptions/AzureOpenAIOptions bound from appsettings.json + DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT-specific json + user-secrets + env vars; DI registrar wired into Spectre.Console.Cli CommandApp.

Npgsql data source with UseVector(), embedded Schema.sql, and idempotent SchemaMigrator wired into Program.cs startup (skipped when no connection string configured). Verified end-to-end against a local Postgres 17 + pgvector instance: extensions, ai_doc_chunk table, HNSW/trgm/GIN indexes, and unique constraint all created correctly, and a second run was a no-op.

Added 'status' command (Segrep/Commands/StatusCommand.cs): checks PostgreSQL via NpgsqlConnection.OpenAsync and Azure Document Intelligence/OpenAI via HttpClient GET, renders results in a Spectre.Console table with Configured/Reachable/Detail columns. Verified all three states live (reachable, unreachable via DNS failure and connection refused, not configured).

Added 'configure' command (Segrep/Commands/ConfigureCommand.cs) that walks PostgreSQL, Azure Document Intelligence, and Azure OpenAI one at a time via Spectre.Console prompts (Confirm + TextPrompt, masked Secret() for API keys, blank-to-keep-current semantics), writing only changed values to dotnet user-secrets via a new UserSecretsStore helper (Segrep/Configuration/UserSecretsStore.cs) - never touches appsettings.json. Also fixed a startup bug uncovered during testing: the schema migration runner in Program.cs crashed the whole CLI (including 'status') when Postgres was configured but unreachable; now caught and surfaced as a warning.

Already satisfied by Segrep/Configuration/AzureDocumentIntelligenceOptions.cs (added during App Configuration work): Endpoint + ApiKey bound from config, plus an IsConfigured capability flag for the future ingestion path (rc4) to gate on. No additional code needed; verified build is clean and the flag is already exercised by the status/configure commands.

Already satisfied by Segrep/Configuration/AzureOpenAIOptions.cs (added during App Configuration work): Endpoint + ApiKey + ChatDeploymentName bound from config, already exercised by status/configure commands. No additional code needed for the Interpreter chat model's config surface; the Interpreter Model task (nh6) can consume this directly.

Added Segrep/Configuration/EmbeddingModelOptions.cs (Dimensions=1536, BatchSize=16 defaults) bound from a new 'EmbeddingModel' appsettings.json section and registered in AppConfiguration.AddAppConfiguration. Endpoint/ApiKey/EmbeddingDeploymentName were already covered by the shared AzureOpenAIOptions (same Azure OpenAI resource backs both chat and embedding deployments), so this adds only the embedding-specific knobs. Verified binding and env-var override (EmbeddingModel__BatchSize) work via a temporary debug print, then removed it; build is clean.
Added AzureEmbeddingGeneratorFactory + EmbeddingService (Segrep/Embeddings/), ModelName option, batching by EmbeddingModelOptions.BatchSize, Dimensions=1536 via EmbeddingGenerationOptions
Registered DocumentIntelligenceClient singleton in DI via Segrep.DocumentIntelligence.ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddAzureDocumentIntelligence(), wired in Program.cs
Schema, embedding pipeline, and pgvector indexes are all in place; this is the resulting data artifact, not separate code.
HelpCommand implemented; Program.cs enriched with WithDescription/AddExample/SetApplicationVersion; scaffold commands removed. Build clean.
Implemented intent routing + per-document retrieval; verified all questions from questions.md end-to-end

Refactored index command: per-file progress bars, Parallel.ForEachAsync with --parallel cap (default 4), Esc-to-cancel via linked CTS + key listener. Verified multi-file parallel ingest, unchanged path, and single-file run against documents-ingestion/bank-files.
